reason_template: Engine flushes are rarely needed before {mileage_threshold_min} miles. Most manufacturers do not recommend them and they can dislodge sludge that clogs oil passages.
when_actually_needed: If you have sludge buildup from missed oil changes or are buying a used car with unknown maintenance history.

reason_template: Fuel injector cleaning is rarely needed on modern cars that use quality fuel. Most injectors last the life of the vehicle.
when_actually_needed: If you experience rough idle, poor fuel economy, or misfires that diagnostic confirms as injector-related.

reason_template: Most manufacturers recommend coolant flush at 100k miles or every 5 years. Shops frequently push this at 30k miles.
when_actually_needed: At 100k miles or if coolant is discolored, or if you notice overheating or heater problems.

manufacturer_interval: lifetime or 60-100k miles depending on manufacturer
reason_template: Many modern transmissions use lifetime fluid. Shops often push flushes at 30-60k miles. Check your owner's manual first.
when_actually_needed: If your transmission slips, hesitates, or if fluid appears dark and burnt. Verify manufacturer recommendation first.

reason_template: Nitrogen tire fill provides no meaningful benefit over regular air for street vehicles. The pressure retention difference is negligible.
when_actually_needed: Only useful for racing applications where precise pressure is critical at high temperatures.

reason_template: Cabin air filters are often charged at 3-5x the retail price when bundled with oil changes. A $12 filter becomes a $50 service.
when_actually_needed: Every 15,000-25,000 miles or annually. Easy DIY — takes 5 minutes with no tools on most vehicles.

reason_template: Alignment is often added to brake jobs without checking if it's needed. Alignment is only required if the vehicle pulls to one side, tires show uneven wear, or steering is off-center.
when_actually_needed: After suspension work, hitting a large pothole, or if tires show uneven wear or vehicle pulls to one side.

reason_template: Serpentine belts typically last 60,000-100,000 miles. Replacement before 60k miles is likely unnecessary unless the belt shows visible cracking or fraying.
when_actually_needed: If the belt is cracked, frayed, glazed, or squealing, or at 60k-100k miles as preventive maintenance.

reason_template: Power steering flush is almost never recommended by manufacturers and typically unnecessary unless the fluid is dark or contaminated.
when_actually_needed: Only if fluid is visibly contaminated or if you've had a power steering component replaced.

reason_template: Engine air filters are frequently charged at 3-4x retail when bundled with oil changes. A $15 filter becomes a $50 charge.
when_actually_needed: Every 15,000-30,000 miles or when visibly dirty. Easy DIY — usually no tools required.
